scope.summary: DI6 preserves successful oracle_checkpoint and oracle_undo_to responses across late deadline handling while leaving previews and effectless bodies cancellable.
out_of_scope: No live Oracle deadline-injection lane is claimed; this close is pinned by the dispatcher unit tests that exercise the terminal-effect classifier directly.
No changes to dashboard HTTP operator files or session-recycle paths are included.

entry_points.name: OracleDispatcher late-deadline terminal-effect classifier
entry_points.trace: crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs:12020-12040 classifies successful oracle_checkpoint responses carrying a checkpoint string and oracle_undo_to responses carrying a statement string as terminal effects, including full rollback responses whose undone_to is null.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp --lib successful_checkpoint_and_undo_are_terminal_effects -- --nocapture
entry_points.trace: Re-run after source verification on 2026-07-22: 1 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored. The test covers successful checkpoint, named undo, and full undo response shapes.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp --lib previews_and_effectless_bodies_stay_cancellable -- --nocapture
entry_points.trace: Re-run on 2026-07-22: 1 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored. The test proves oracle_preview_dml and malformed/effectless checkpoint/undo bodies do not become terminal effects.

entry_points.name: git diff --quiet 3fa4fc06a7bfb946dc6ad6aea7f6b08b344e4057..332c2c658d259babcd976e32084313b045d3b8b7 -- crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs crates/oraclemcp/src/registry.rs
entry_points.trace: Exit 0: HEAD advanced during verification only via tracker sync; the DI6 source content at evidence HEAD is identical to the source content tested.

limitations: Scoped-test close only; no full workspace gate or live Oracle deadline race fixture is claimed.
The shared checkout had unrelated dirty files outside this scope; the scoped source paths and evidence files were clean before this evidence was written.
